name: Mitochondrial delivery and transplantation surveillance pass for June 3, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Mitochondrial Delivery and Transplantation since the June 3, 2026 bootstrap publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: No material post-publication delta was found for the public outlook. NCT02851758 remains recruiting with no results section, NCT04998357 remains recruiting with no results section, and PubMed same-day delta checks found no new treated-human aging, functional, safety, or efficacy result that would change the low-confidence animal_signal baseline. The pass did identify pre-publication-window source-completeness issues: PMID 42171348 reports xenogeneic mitochondrial transplantation improving selected age-associated phenotypes in mice, PMID 41986718 reports a targeted mitochondrial-delivery platform with human and mouse cell-type targeting, and several 2026 reviews or adjacent studies clarify cardiac, kidney, stroke, fertility, and delivery-technology boundaries. These should be handled as coverage repair rather than silently staged as a surveillance delta.

materiality_decision.rationale: The direct post-publication surveillance question found no public-update delta, but pre-window searches surfaced potentially decision-relevant source-completeness gaps, including a direct animal-aging xeno-MT paper and a targeted-delivery technology paper that were not in the published baseline. Those are not post-publication deltas, so the right next step is a coverage-repair pass that audits recent primary papers, reviews, and taxonomy boundaries before changing public records.

search_log.summary: Record remains RECRUITING, last posted update January 5, 2026, with estimated primary completion June 2027 and no results section.
Record remains RECRUITING, last posted update May 21, 2025, with estimated primary completion April 29, 2026, estimated completion October 29, 2026, and no results section.

Broad registry search did not find a new posted-results treated-human aging study for this track. It did surface adjacent no-results or wrong-scope records, including reproductive and organ-transplant contexts that should be considered only during taxonomy or coverage repair.

search_log.query: (mitochondrial transplantation OR mitochondria transplantation OR mitochondrial transfer OR mitotherapy) AND (aging OR ageing OR aged OR elderly OR senescence OR ischemia OR stroke OR cardiac), same-day Entrez delta
search_log.summary: The same-day Entrez-date check returned PMID 42229862, a June 1, 2026 narrative review of mitochondrial dynamics and endogenous mitochondrial transfer in the aging brain. It is context, not a new treated-intervention result.

search_log.query: 2026 mitochondrial transplantation, mitochondrial transfer, and mitotherapy terms with aging, aged, elderly, senescence, ischemia, stroke, and cardiac terms
search_log.summary: Found no post-publication primary result requiring public update, but did identify pre-window coverage-repair candidates including PMID 42171348, PMID 41986718, PMID 42123597, PMID 41643906, and PMID 42129850.

excluded_sources.title: Cell-specific energy crisis in the ageing brain: Mitochondrial dynamics drives neuron-glia metabolic uncoupling and neurodegeneration
excluded_sources.reason: Same-day indexed review discusses endogenous mitochondrial transfer and aging-brain bioenergetics, but it is not a new treated mitochondrial-delivery intervention result and does not change the public stage, momentum, or support map.

excluded_sources.title: Xenogeneic Mitochondrial Transplantation Improves Selected Age-Associated Phenotypes in Mice
excluded_sources.reason: Potentially decision-relevant direct animal-aging evidence, but it predates the June 3 publication window and should be evaluated in a coverage-repair pass rather than treated as a surveillance delta.

excluded_sources.title: Cell-type-targeted mitochondrial transplantation rescues cell degeneration
excluded_sources.reason: Potentially important delivery-technology and human-cell/mouse in vivo context, but it predates the publication window and is not direct aging efficacy; coverage repair should decide whether and how it affects technology and targeting boundaries.

excluded_sources.title: Therapeutic Impact of Mitochondrial Transplants for Cardiovascular Diseases
excluded_sources.reason: Recent cardiovascular review predates the surveillance window and is synthesis context, not new primary aging or older-adult efficacy evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Autologous bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell mitochondrial transplantation in recurrent assisted reproductive technology failure: a randomized controlled trial
excluded_sources.reason: Human RCT context is reproductive/ART failure rather than healthy-aging or LEV-relevant treated-human function; it may inform taxonomy boundaries during coverage repair but does not change this track outlook.
No checked registry result supplied posted treated-human aging, functional, safety, or efficacy outcomes after the baseline publication.

notes: Triage selected this track as the next distinct surveillance target after ecosystem-replacement.
No coverage assessment exists yet for this track; triage separately listed coverage-assessment backfill as the next same-track maintenance task.
The public outlook remains low-confidence animal_signal because no checked post-publication source reports treated-human aging efficacy or durable functional benefit.
PMID 42171348 and PMID 41986718 look more important than ordinary excluded context, but they are source-completeness and boundary issues, not post-publication deltas.

Coverage repair should audit recent primary papers, delivery-technology papers, cardiovascular/kidney/stroke reviews, reproductive mitochondrial-transfer boundaries, and whether the current public support map should add a xeno-MT animal-aging finding.
next_actions: Run coverage repair for mitochondrial delivery and transplantation before the next ordinary surveillance pass.
During coverage repair, decide whether PMID 42171348 and PMID 41986718 warrant staged public source, study, finding, or outlook-support updates.
Backfill a coverage assessment after the coverage-repair decision so planning state records the category-level coverage verdict and known gaps.
